name: Beefsteak and Kidney Pie
recipeIngredient: 4 small Veal or Baby Beef Kidneys
1 1/2 lb Rump or Round Steak (to be cut into 1-inch pieces)
1 cup Claret or other Dry Red Wine
1/2 cup Celery, chopped (including the tender leafy tops)
Pastry of puff-paste, pie crust, or biscuit dough (to cover casserole)
recipeInstructions:
1. Separate the kidneys with a sharp knife, discarding all gristly portions and fat. Sprinkle with salt.
2. Cover with claret, add bay leaves, sliced onion, pepper. Let marinate for 2 hours.
3. Pound the steak with flour, then cut into 1-inch pieces.
4. Brown suet or heat bacon drippings in an iron skillet. Add diced onion and cook until clear; remove onion from the skillet.
5. Over medium heat, brown steak well. Next, drain the kidneys, reserving the marinade; dredge with flour and brown, stirring carefully.
6. Add 1 pint (or less) hot water to the casserole, stir well, add chopped celery and parsley, and marjoram. Mix all well and transfer to the casserole from which you intend to serve the pie. Strain into it the marinade.
7. Cover with a tight lid and bake at 325°F for about 1 hour.
8. Remove from oven. Brown mushrooms in bacon drippings, and if needed, thicken with flour mixed in cold water and stirred carefully to avoid lumps. Add the mushrooms to the casserole mixture.
9. Cover with a crust of puff-paste, pie crust, or biscuit dough and return to 400°F oven, baking until brown, about 20 to 30 minutes. Serve at once.
